title: Albert Einstein
summary: Albert Einstein was a German-born theoretical physicist who developed the theory of relativity and won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

title: Coldkeys (Bittensor)
summary: The security-critical key that controls TAO and stake; used for financial transactions like stake, unstake, and transfers.

title: Dynamic TAO (dTAO)
summary: An in-depth explanation of Dynamic TAO, Bittensor’s evolving tokenomics and governance model that decentralizes emissions and decision-making across subnets.

title: Earth
summary: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only known place in the universe where life exists.

title: Gravity
summary: Gravity is a fundamental interaction that causes mutual attraction between all objects with mass or energy.

title: Hotkeys (Bittensor)
summary: The operational key used by miners, validators, and subnet actors to register, submit weights, and perform day-to-day on-chain actions.

title: Isaac Newton
summary: Sir Isaac Newton was an English mathematician, physicist, and astronomer who formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation.

title: Mining and Validating in Bittensor
summary: A factual overview of miners and validators in Bittensor, including roles, registration, evaluation, rewards, and how emissions are distributed.

title: Moon
summary: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite and the fifth-largest moon in the Solar System; its gravitational influence drives Earth's ocean tides and stabilizes the planet's axial tilt.

title: Physics
summary: Physics is the natural science that studies matter, energy, and the fundamental forces of nature.

title: Staking and Delegation (TAO)
summary: How TAO holders stake and delegate to validators using Bittensor tooling, including concepts, minimums, and operational cautions.

title: Subnet Creation and Incentive Mechanisms
summary: How subnet creators define tasks and incentive mechanisms that guide validator evaluations and miner rewards on Bittensor.

title: Taopedia
summary: Taopedia is a wiki-style knowledge base organized as Markdown/MDX pages with rich frontmatter, internal links, categories, search, and an infobox layout.

title: Bittensor Wallets
summary: What a Bittensor wallet is, what it stores, and how it is used to prove identity and sign transactions on the network.

title: Wallets, Coldkeys, and Hotkeys
summary: Understanding Bittensor wallets and the roles of coldkeys and hotkeys for security, identity, and operations.

title: Yuma Consensus
summary: An in-depth explanation of Yuma Consensus, the mechanism that aggregates validator weights to translate evaluations into incentive allocations across miners and validators.
